% -------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions and basic predicates
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..N-1) :- n_cols(N).

% Output grid is 3x3
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper predicates for counting within groups
% -------------------------------------------------
% For groups 0 and 1 (even output rows) we count earlier rows and,
% for a cell in the same row, earlier columns.
% For group 2 (odd output row) we count later rows and earlier columns
%  this yields the reversed order needed for the odd row.
rank_normal(G,Rin,Cin,Idx) :-
    Rin_mod = G,
    N1 = #count{ X : input(X,_,_,X_mod) = G, X < Rin },
    N2 = #count{ Y : input(Rin,Y,_,_) = true, Y < Cin },
    Idx = N1 + N2.

% For group 2 we use reversed row order and lefttoright column order.
rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,Idx) :-
    Rin_mod = 2,
    N1 = #count{ X : input(X,_,_,X_mod) = 2, X > Rin },
    N2 = #count{ Y : input(Rin,Y,_,_) = true, Y < Cin },
    Idx = N1 + N2.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3. Map output cells to input cells according to the rule
% -------------------------------------------------
% Row 0 (group 1, snake index = column)
output(0,0,Color) :- Rin_mod = 1, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,0), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(0,1,Color) :- Rin_mod = 1, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,1), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(0,2,Color) :- Rin_mod = 1, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,2), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).

% Row 1 (group 2, snake index is 2column)
output(1,2,Color) :- Rin_mod = 2, rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,0), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(1,1,Color) :- Rin_mod = 2, rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,1), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(1,0,Color) :- Rin_mod = 2, rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,2), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).

% Row 2 (group 0)
output(2,0,Color) :- Rin_mod = 0, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,0), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(2,1,Color) :- Rin_mod = 0, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,1), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(2,2,Color) :- Rin_mod = 0, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,2), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4. Allow zero colour when no input cell matches the required rank
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :-
    Rin_mod = (R+1) mod 3,
    N = #count{ Rin : Rin_mod = (R+1) mod 3,
               #count{ X : input(X,_,_,X_mod) = (R+1) mod 3,
                         (X < Rin) or (X = Rin, Y < Cin) } = #count{ X : input(X,_,_,X_mod) = (R+1) mod 3 } - 1 }.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5. Choose exactly one colour for each output cell
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions and basic predicates
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..N-1) :- n_cols(N).

% Output grid is 3x3
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper predicates for counting within groups
% -------------------------------------------------
rank_normal(G,Rin,Cin,Idx) :-
    Rin_mod = G,
    N1 = #count{ X : input(X,_,_,X_mod) = G, X < Rin },
    N2 = #count{ Y : input(Rin,Y,_,_) = true, Y < Cin },
    Idx = N1 + N2.

rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,Idx) :-
    Rin_mod = 2,

% -------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions and basic predicates
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..N-1) :- n_cols(N).

% Output grid is 3x3
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper predicates for counting within groups
% -------------------------------------------------
% For groups 0 and 1 (even output rows) we count earlier rows and,
% for a cell in the same row, earlier columns.
% For group 2 (odd output row) we count later rows and earlier columns
%  this yields the reversed order needed for the odd row.
rank_normal(G,Rin,Cin,Idx) :-
    Rin_mod = G,
    N1 = #count{ X : input(X,_,_,X_mod) = G, X < Rin },
    N2 = #count{ Y : input(Rin,Y,_,_) = true, Y < Cin },
    Idx = N1 + N2.

% For group 2 we use reversed row order and lefttoright column order.
rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,Idx) :-
    Rin_mod = 2,
    N1 = #count{ X : input(X,_,_,X_mod) = 2, X > Rin },
    N2 = #count{ Y : input(Rin,Y,_,_) = true, Y < Cin },
    Idx = N1 + N2.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3. Map output cells to input cells according to the rule
% -------------------------------------------------
% Row 0 (group 1, snake index = column)
output(0,0,Color) :- Rin_mod = 1, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,0), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(0,1,Color) :- Rin_mod = 1, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,1), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(0,2,Color) :- Rin_mod = 1, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,2), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).

% Row 1 (group 2, snake index is 2column)
output(1,2,Color) :- Rin_mod = 2, rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,0), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(1,1,Color) :- Rin_mod = 2, rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,1), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(1,0,Color) :- Rin_mod = 2, rank_rev_2(Rin,Cin,2), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).

% Row 2 (group 0)
output(2,0,Color) :- Rin_mod = 0, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,0), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(2,1,Color) :- Rin_mod = 0, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,1), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).
output(2,2,Color) :- Rin_mod = 0, rank_normal(Rin,Cin,2), Color_of_input(Rin,Cin,Color).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4. Allow zero colour when no input cell matches the required rank
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :-
    Rin_mod = (R+1) mod 3,

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
